symbols: Christ Child's blessing gesture — divine authority even in infancy
Mary's downcast eyes — humility and contemplation
cherub heads with wings — heavenly presence
landscape with city — earthly realm under divine care
description: A Venetian Renaissance Madonna and Child painting. The Virgin Mary sits in three-quarter view, wearing a white veil, red dress, and dark blue mantle. She holds the infant Christ who sits on her lap with one hand raised in a gesture of blessing. Two winged cherub heads float in the sky above. Behind them, a panoramic landscape stretches to a hilltop town on the left and trees and buildings on the right. The sky shows clouds and soft evening light. The painting has a warm, gentle quality characteristic of Carpaccio's devotional works.
theological_description: The Madonna and Blessing Child is a devotional image type presenting the Christ Child already exercising His divine authority through the gesture of benediction. Despite His infancy, the blessing hand reveals His identity as the incarnate God — fully divine even while fully human. Mary's tender yet reserved expression reflects her role as Theotokos (God-bearer), the one through whom the eternal Word took flesh. The cherubim overhead affirm the heavenly nature of the Child she holds. This image type served as a focus for private devotion, inviting the viewer to receive Christ's blessing through contemplation of the sacred image.

scripture_refs.reason: Mary giving birth to her firstborn son — the Incarnation depicted
God sent His Son, born of a woman — the theology of the Incarnation
The firstborn over all creation, in whom all things hold together — the blessing Child's divine nature
